intro: Illigera vespertilio, also known as Bat Wing or Evening Primrose, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Hernandiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Illigera vespertilio include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to treat respiratory issues, fever, and other health conditions.
